text: In the stillness of these spaces, the sounds of an auction echo only in my imagination: the rapid, rhythmic cadence of the auctioneer, words tumbling fast and urgent; the sharp punctuations of bids called out, bodies shifting tensely with anticipation; an undercurrent of restless energy, like a murmur that surges and retracts. Yet here, with soft carpets, scattered bean bags, and the gentle hum of distant monitors, I feel only a hushed expectancy—waiting for the torrent of voices that never comes. I feel strangely suspended, aware that so many possibilities of sound could fill this emptiness, but instead there is a contemplative calm.
